case_name: Annie L. Zephaniah v. Georgia Clinic, P.C.

summary.brief: Annie L. Zephaniah v. Georgia Clinic, P.C.. Medical malpractice case involving cardiology.

evidence_index.text: Zephaniah went to the defendant’s office “for a routine blood draw checkup.”
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Zephaniah sat “in the lab chair in anticipation for Dina to draw [her] blood as usual” and “extended [her] right arm out in anticipation of the blood draw.”
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The needle pierced the tendon bone . . . , [the technician] kept pushing the needle [until] it couldn’t go any further, striking a nerve that immediately fired an electric shock that radiated through [her] arm, into [her] elbow and [her] . . . brain.
evidence_index.speaker: Zephaniah's account

evidence_index.text: Zephaniah claims that she saw a doctor the following week because of “the pain in [her] forearm” and inability to sleep.
evidence_index.speaker: Zephaniah's account

evidence_index.text: The trial court nonetheless erred by granting summary judgment on Zephaniah’s entire ordinary negligence claim because the instantaneous pain she experienced due to the alleged negligence of the technician during the blood draw is “perfectly capable of resolution by ordinary people using their common knowledge and experience.”
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: In a medical context, consent encompasses two distinct legal principles: “basic” consent and “informed” consent.
evidence_index.speaker: Court analysis

evidence_index.text: Zephaniah held her arm “sling-like because of the pain in [her] forearm.”
evidence_index.speaker: Zephaniah's account

evidence_index.text: Judgment affirmed in part, reversed in part.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient went to the clinic for a routine blood draw. During the procedure, the technician inserted the needle incorrectly, causing immediate pain and nerve injury.

focused_exam.arm: Patient held her arm sling-like due to pain in the forearm

initial_state.initial_working_problem: Pain and potential nerve injury following blood draw
critical_findings: Immediate pain and electric shock sensation radiating through arm

requestables.name: Follow-up with doctor
requestables.description: Consultation for pain in forearm following blood draw

reveal.result_summary: Patient reported ongoing pain and numbness in the right arm
reveal.decision_impact: Should prompt further investigation and management

timeline_phases.name: Clinic Visit for Blood Draw
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents for a routine blood draw

key_events.event: Patient arrives at clinic for blood draw
Technician attempts blood draw from forearm

timeline_phases.name: Follow-up Consultation
timeline_phases.description: Patient follows up with doctor due to pain

key_events.event: Patient reports ongoing pain and numbness

decision_points.prompt: How should the blood draw be performed?

options.description: Use the back of the hand for blood draw as per patient's history
options.clinical_reasoning: Patient indicated that blood is usually drawn from the back of the hand
Technician chose to draw from the forearm despite patient's history

actual_action_defendant.description: Technician attempted to draw blood from the forearm
actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Technician did not follow patient's usual procedure

expected_action_court.description: Blood should have been drawn from the back of the hand

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Technician did not follow the patient's usual procedure, leading to nerve injury
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Blood should have been drawn from the back of the hand
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Standard of care as established by patient's history

patient_outcome.description: Patient experienced ongoing pain and numbness in the right arm
patient_outcome.long_term_effects: Chronic pain, numbness, and nerve damage

key_findings: Technician's negligence in performing blood draw
Summary judgment reversed in part for ordinary negligence claim

malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Proper technique would have avoided nerve injury

factual_timeline.event: Patient arrives at clinic for blood draw

court_findings.finding: Technician's negligence in performing blood draw
court_findings.legal_significance: Reversal of summary judgment for ordinary negligence
